season was the 78th season of the Ligue de Football Professionnel first division since its establishment. It started on 7 August 2015 and concluded on 14 May 2016. [Paris Saint-Germain](/wiki/Paris_Saint-Germain_F.C. "Paris Saint-Germain F.C.") were the defending champions, and retained the title with a 9–0 win at [Troyes](/wiki/Troyes_AC "Troyes AC") on 13 March.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It was their fourth consecutive Ligue 1 title.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The **2015–16 Ligue 1** season was the 78th season of the Ligue de Football Professionnel first division since its establishment. It started on 7 August 2015 and concluded on 14 May 2016. [Paris Saint-Germain](/wiki/Paris_Saint-Germain_F.C. "Paris Saint-Germain F.C.") were the defending champions, and retained the title with a 9–0 win at [Troyes](/wiki/Troyes_AC "Troyes AC") on 13 March.[[3]](#cite_note-3) It was their fourth consecutive Ligue 1 title.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=2015%E2%80%9316_Ligue_1&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Teams")]

There were 20 clubs in the league, with three promoted teams from [Ligue 2](/wiki/Ligue_2 "Ligue 2") replacing the three teams that were relegated from [Ligue 1](/wiki/Ligue_1 "Ligue 1") following the [2014–15 season](/wiki/2014%E2%80%9315_Ligue_1 "2014–15 Ligue 1"). All clubs that secured Ligue 1 status for the season were subject to approval by the [DNCG](/wiki/Direction_Nationale_du_Contr%C3%B4le_de_Gestion "Direction Nationale du Contrôle de Gestion") before becoming eligible to participate. Originally, only two teams were planned to be relegated at the end of the season.[[5]](#cite_note-5) However, this proposal was appealed and eventually overturned, so as in past seasons, three teams are to be relegated.[[6]](#cite_note-6) Evian, Metz and Lens were relegated to Ligue 2 at the conclusion of the 2014–15 season. Troyes, Gazelec Ajaccio and Angers were promoted to the top level. Troyes returned after being relegated in [2012–13 season](/wiki/2012%E2%80%9313_Ligue_1 "2012–13 Ligue 1"). Gazelec Ajaccio secured their second consecutive promotion and joined Ligue 1 for the first time in the club's history. Angers returned to the top level after 21 years.
